summary: Frontend-only testing of TinkerCAD-style anchored ruler tool (iter 60). 13 acceptance tests executed using a hybrid of real toolbar clicks + programmatic store.setRulerAnchor() (as R3F mesh-clicks are flaky in headless Chromium per agent context note). 12/13 fully pass; T8 has a latent state-cleanup bug.

ui_bugs.issue: Toggling the toolbar button OFF does not clear rulerAnchor from the store — only rulerMode is flipped. Visually correct (HUD/chips are gated on rulerMode so they disappear), but if the user toggles the button back ON the stale anchor from the previous session reappears unexpectedly. Spec explicitly states: 'Toggling the toolbar button OFF clears the anchor AND turns mode off'. Fix: change onClick to () => { setRulerMode(!rulerMode); if (rulerMode) clearRulerAnchor(); } (clear when transitioning ON->OFF). Verified via window.__forgeStore.getState().rulerAnchor after the toggle — it was still { worldPoint:[0,0,0], objId:'cube-…' } instead of null.

action_items: Optional: in EditRow.jsx, on the ruler-anchor-mode-btn onClick, also call clearRulerAnchor() when transitioning from ON->OFF so the stale anchor doesn't resurrect when re-toggled. (One-line fix.)
critical_code_review_comments: rulerAnchor.js / Viewport.jsx: nearestCorner uses Euclidean distance over the 8 corner candidates — this is the right choice and matches the spec 'snap to the visually-nearest corner'.
store.js: cascade cleanup in removeObject / removeSelected / replaceObjects correctly clears rulerAnchor when its objId is removed. Verified live in T9.
store.js: cycleRulerAxes uses a fixed 4-element rotation array — clean. Verified T5 sequence matches xyz->x->y->z->xyz exactly.
useToolbarShortcuts.js Escape handler clears rulerAnchor first (one layer per Esc) — verified T7.

Inconsistency between setRulerMode (mode-only setter) and the toolbar button intent (mode off => mode off + clear anchor). Suggest either (a) make setRulerMode(false) also null rulerAnchor, or (b) handle it at the button. Currently neither is done.
seed_data_creation: Programmatically seeded scenes via window.__forgeStore: (cube,sphere) with mixed +/- offsets; (cube,sphere,cylinder) for cascade tests. clearScene() between tests. No persisted backend data.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All ruler-anchor data-testids (ruler-anchor-mode-btn, ruler-anchor-hud, ruler-cycle-axes-btn, ruler-clear-anchor-btn, ruler-offset-chip-<id>, ruler-offset-x/y/z-<id>) are wired correctly and present in DOM. Programmatic anchor placement via window.__forgeStore.getState().setRulerAnchor(...) is the most reliable test technique — R3F mesh-clicks are flaky in headless. Workspace URL is /workspace (not root). To toggle ruler mode programmatically use setRulerMode(true/false). To verify cascade-on-delete use removeObject(id). Signed format uses Unicode minus '−' (U+2212), not ASCII '-' — check for both when validating values.

test_results_detail.T4_offset_chip_for_others_signed: PASS (e.g. Sphere chip: X −3.00 mm, Y +0.00 mm, Z +4.00 mm — mixed signs confirmed; anchored cube has NO chip)
test_results_detail.T5_axis_cycle_xyz_x_y_z_xyz: PASS (sequence + DOM presence/absence of x/y/z spans verified exactly)
test_results_detail.T8_toolbar_toggle_off_clears_anchor: PARTIAL — mode_off=true, hud_gone=true, BUT anchor_cleared=false. Visually correct, latent bug.
test_results_detail.T10_cascade_delete_other_part: PASS (anchor preserved, only deleted object's chip removed)
test_results_detail.T11_live_update_on_position_change: PASS (X +15.00 mm -> X +70.00 mm after position update)
